capabilityProfile.assumption: The mission-force construct is public; exact contingency package, access, marker, and capacity are scenario assumptions.
Observed theater role represented as a single service node; it does not create a generic space-attack resource.

nodes.name: Multi-Domain Effects Battalion — Cyber / EW Element

capabilityProfile.assumption: The combined package and values are analytical abstractions, not an exact deployed formation.
The package is a theater-level capability aggregate; location and capacity are illustrative.

nodes.name: Taiwan Mission Partner Cyber Defense Package

capabilityProfile.assumption: Availability, authorities, access, and capacity are scenario assumptions; the package does not imply unrestricted network access.
This aggregate models reversible spectrum activity and does not imply destructive counterspace or an Australian deployment.
Japanese support requires an independent political, legal, and operational decision.
The service is an aggregate theater function; marker and capacity are illustrative.
The role is public; the contingency package, partner access, marker, and capacity are scenario assumptions.

nodes.name: U.S. Space Cyber Mission Assurance Package

capabilityProfile.assumption: This is an abstract mission-assurance service, not a claimed company or Japanese deployment.
Observed responsibilities are represented as one national command-service node; marker and wartime organization are illustrative.
A mature distributed defense data fabric is a 2040 scenario assumption.
Public resilience direction supports the service class; military topology and marker are illustrative.
Civil and defense service integration is a scenario assumption.
This aggregates contracted emergency access; provider participation, terminals, bandwidth, and wartime availability are conditional.
A mature coalition gateway and data-release regime are explicit 2040 scenario assumptions.
Observed mission responsibilities are aggregated into one capacity pool; team count and readiness are not asserted.
Cross-sector operational integration and capacity are scenario assumptions.

nodes.name: Clean Backup and Service Recovery Layer

capabilityProfile.assumption: Automated assistance and recovery depth are 2040 assumptions; human authorization remains required.
The mission is public; the aggregate node and capacity are scenario abstractions.
Exact systems, dispersal, authorities, range, and capacity are not public facts.
Sovereign and contracted imagery are aggregated as a future service; access and survivability are not guaranteed.

nodes.name: Satellite Ground and Mission Operations Service

capabilityProfile.assumption: The node represents a distributed service, not a single targetable facility.
This capability affects trust and decision quality and does not generate generic cyber damage.

nodes.name: Public Alert and Trusted Distribution Network

capabilityProfile.assumption: The public service class is observed; defense integration and wartime behavior are scenario assumptions.
Service depends on contracts, provider willingness, terminals, bandwidth, policy, and technical reachability.

nodes.name: Overseas Cloud / CDN and International Transit

capabilityProfile.assumption: Service is commercial and conditional; loss should degrade availability rather than imply physical destruction.
Seamless coalition interoperability is not assumed; this node represents an authorized, controlled 2040 service.
Military, allied, and commercial services are aggregated; Taiwan access is not automatic.
Japanese participation and network access require independent political, legal, and operational decisions.
Australian participation and network access require independent government and operational decisions.
